governing_rules: No entry without all seven required fields
No silent edits — every change is logged with ISO timestamp
No thread may silently supersede another thread's controlling artifact
Status terms require SealForge receipts before public use
Health and sensory claims require E-tier receipt (E1 minimum, E5 for clinical)
Ellari holds final release authority — public_release_allowed must be true
Registry memory is the most structured version, not the most beautiful version

entries.title: Three Axioms — Level 0 Grammar
entries.claim_boundary: The three axioms govern all SLA structural claims. They are not empirical claims — they are definitional constraints on what SLA counts as a structural state.
allowed_to_prove: Coherence Axiom: systems occupy coherent, destabilized, or collapsed states
T3 Rule: T3→F1 blocked; bypass = Type C
R1-D Principle: R1-D > Type C
not_allowed_to_prove: Universality of these axioms across all possible structural systems
Clinical application without E-tier evidence chain

entries.title: R1 Availability Taxonomy — A/B/C/D
entries.claim_boundary: The R1 taxonomy distinguishes four structural states of return availability. Each is formally distinct with different governing implications.
allowed_to_prove: R1-A: structurally unavailable return — the system conditions preclude return process
R1-B: available return missed within the structural window
R1-C: false repair — the appearance of F1 without return process
R1-D: directional movement — forward without arrival claim
not_allowed_to_prove: Value judgment about which R1 state is preferable
Clinical prescription for moving between R1 states

entries.title: First Three-Domain Alignment — Text + Music + Visual
entries.claim_boundary: Pulled Too Late returns the same SLA grammar arc when analyzed as literary text, as musical structure, and as visual composition. Not a claim of equivalence across domains — a claim of structural alignment within this corpus.
allowed_to_prove: Corpus-aligned structural grammar across three domains for this specimen
T3 entry point, voicemail degradation arc, and R1-B taxonomy consistent across media
not_allowed_to_prove: Universal cross-domain alignment
That the grammar was intended by the author across domains

entries.title: Pulled Too Late · Primary Scan Receipt
entries.claim_boundary: Cross-domain structural alignment within corpus. Not a claim of equivalence with real events. Not a clinical reading.
allowed_to_prove: The SLA grammar arc F1→T3→blocked_R1 is present in the text
168-second window constitutes R1-B structural condition
not_allowed_to_prove: Identity of real individuals
Authorial intent beyond documented author's note
Clinical or psychological assessment of characters

entries.title: Sensory Modulation Grammar
entries.claim_boundary: Evidence-gated sensory grammar across 8 domains. E1–E5 tier system. CLINICAL HARDGATE — not for public release until E3+ expert review.
not_allowed_to_prove: Clinical efficacy
Therapeutic benefit
Sensory regulation outcomes without E3+ receipt
entries.hardgate_note: Requires expert review before ANY public claim. E5 required for therapeutic claim.

entries.title: What Kuhn Described — A Formal Grammar for Scientific Revolution
entries.claim_boundary: Cross-domain structural alignment between SLA and Kuhn's Structure of Scientific Revolutions. P1/T3 distinction as central contribution. Not a claim of equivalence with Kuhn's work or replacement for peer review.
allowed_to_prove: Kuhn's phenomenological description of scientific revolution maps to SLA's formal P1/T3 distinction with structural precision
Normal science period = F1+I1 holding
Anomaly = T1 entry
Crisis = T3
Paradigm shift = T3→◇ (incommensurability = T3→F1 blocked)
Revolution = R1 (new paradigm, not F1 restored)
not_allowed_to_prove: Universality
That Kuhn intended the SLA grammar
Replacement for academic philosophy of science peer review
Clinical application
entries.sealforge_note: Seal required before PhilSci-Archive submission. SHA-256 of final .docx needed.

entries.title: Collapse Music Notation — Framework Registry Entry
entries.claim_boundary: CMN is a formal notation extension demonstrating cross-tradition structural alignment across six specimens. Not a claim of universality. Not a claim of therapeutic efficacy.
allowed_to_prove: T3→F1 block appears independently in Hindustani classical law (rasa bhanga) and Western classical composition (Shostakovich letter evidence)
The SLA grammar accounts for all five specimen arcs without modification
CMN provides a formal notation system for structural states in music
not_allowed_to_prove: Universality — six specimens, three traditions
That therapeutic benefit follows from governed listening
That the grammar was known to any tradition before SLA named it

entries.title: 11:02 — A Collection · Release State
entries.claim_boundary: Six-piece literary collection demonstrating the full R1 taxonomy. Authorial confirmations required before seal.
allowed_to_prove: The collection demonstrates all four R1 subtypes in sequence
Track iv (Fine) is structurally distinct from Track 0 (The Week Before) despite surface similarity
Track vi (Walking) ends in R1-D — directional movement without arrival
not_allowed_to_prove: Identity of real individuals
Authorial intent beyond the documented author's note
Therapeutic or clinical benefit

entries.title: Canon Memory Protocol — Governance Axiom
entries.claim_boundary: The canon is not what anyone remembers. The canon is what the registry says when you read it.
allowed_to_prove: The registry is the authoritative source for all governed claims
Memory without receipt becomes mythology
canonical_lines: The registry is not what you remember. The registry is what it says when you read it.
A canon without memory becomes mythology. A canon with memory becomes infrastructure.
